observe: Closer isn't always better. Closer is faster and smaller. That trade never goes away.
fault: You reached for something and the fast layer didn't have it. Now you pay for the layer that does.
repair: Walk the pyramid. Registers, cache, memory, disk — feel where the cost changes.
escape: No time to enjoy the view. Name the levels before the miss compounds.

observe: Memory is organized as a hierarchy trading speed against capacity.
fault: A cache miss has occurred, requiring access to a slower memory level.
repair: Examine each level of the hierarchy from fastest to slowest.
escape: Identify all hierarchy levels before time runs out.

hints: Smaller and closer to the CPU always means faster — work outward from there.
